---
params:
  county: "Salt Lake"
  data_dir: "data/remotes"
format:
  typst:
    margin:
      x: 0.4in
      y: 0.35in
    fontsize: 8pt
---

```{=typst}
#set page(flipped: true)
#set text(size: 7pt)
```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(yaml)
library(tinytable)

settings    <- yaml::read_yaml("config/settings.yml")
report_data <- readRDS(file.path(params$data_dir, "report_data.rds"))
tract_maps  <- readRDS(file.path(params$data_dir, "tract_maps.rds"))

county_data <- dplyr::filter(report_data, county == params$county) |>
  dplyr::arrange(GEOID)

county_maps <- tract_maps[county_data$GEOID]
```

```{r}
#| label: save-maps
#| include: false
# Save each tract map to PNG so tinytable can embed them as inline images.
# PNGs are cached in data_dir/map_cache/ — safe to delete and regenerate.
map_dir <- file.path(params$data_dir, "map_cache")
dir.create(map_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

map_paths <- purrr::imap_chr(county_maps, function(map, geoid) {
  path <- file.path(map_dir, paste0(geoid, ".png"))
  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = path,
    plot     = map,
    width    = settings$map_width_in,
    height   = settings$map_height_in,
    dpi      = 200,
    bg       = "white"
  )
  path
})
```

```{r}
#| label: table
tbl_data <- county_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    map           = "",  # placeholder; filled by plot_tt() below
    wc_muc_pct    = round(wc_muc_pct,    1),
    ato_composite = round(ato_composite, 1),
    eru_per_acre  = round(eru_per_acre,  2)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    map,
    GEOID, pop, hh,
    total_acres, oz1_acres, oz1_pct, dev_acres,
    oztoolclassification,
    wc_muc_pct,
    ato_composite,
    sap_planned_hh_per_acre,
    eru_per_acre
  )

col_labels <- c(
  "Map",
  "Tract ID", "Pop.", "HH",
  "Total Acres", "OZ 1.0 Acres", "OZ 1.0 %", "Dev. Acres",
  "OZ Investment Likelihood",
  "MUorC Cov. %",
  "ATO Score (0-100)",
  "SAP HH/Acre",
  "ERU/Acre"
)

tinytable::tt(
  setNames(tbl_data, col_labels),
  notes = "Sources: Federal OZ 2.0 eligibility, Urban Institute, WFRC/MAG regional data. Growth projections 2027-2037. ATO = Access to Opportunities.",
  width = 1
) |>
  tinytable::plot_tt(
    j      = 1,
    images = unname(map_paths),
    height = settings$map_height_in / 10,
    width  = settings$map_width_in  / 10
  ) |>
  tinytable::style_tt(
    fontsize = 6,
    align    = "l"
  )
```
